messages.content: proposer un statut facebook sur le partenariat entre université sousse et napoli lors du forum internation sur le changement climatique tenu à sousse

"Je suis ravi de partager ce moment historique avec vous tous ! L'Université de Sousse s'est engagée dans un partenariat fructueux avec l'Université de Napoli lors du Forum International sur le Changement Climatique tenu à Sousse. Ensemble, nous travaillerons à trouver des solutions innovantes pour lutter contre les défis environnementaux auxquels notre monde est confronté. En unissant nos forces, nous pouvons faire la différence et construire un avenir durable pour les générations à venir. #PartenariatUniversitaire #ChangementClimatiqueEnAction"

"Une nouvelle ère de collaboration internationale pour lutter contre le changement climatique ! L'Université de Sousse et l'Université de Napoli ont officiellement annoncé leur partenariat lors du Forum International sur le Changement Climatique tenu à Sousse. Ensemble, nous travaillerons main dans la main pour trouver des solutions innovantes et durables face à ce défi mondial pressant. Unissons nos efforts, partageons nos connaissances et utilisons notre expertise pour protéger notre planète et assurer un avenir meilleur pour tous. #PartenariatUniversitaire #ChangementClimatique #CollaborationInternationale"

"C'est avec beaucoup d'enthousiasme que je vous annonce le partenariat entre l'Université de Sousse et l'Université de Napoli lors du Forum International sur le Changement Climatique à Sousse. Ensemble, nous sommes déterminés à faire avancer la lutte contre le changement climatique et à trouver des solutions concrètes pour préserver notre environnement. Cette collaboration entre institutions renommées témoigne de notre engagement commun en faveur d'un avenir plus durable. Rejoignez-nous dans cette mission et ensemble, faisons la différence pour notre planète ! #PartenariatUniversitaire #ChangementClimatique #SousseNapoliPartenariat"

reformuler et améliorer : Le forum fournira une plate-forme idéale pour présenter, discuter et mettre en œuvre des idées et des approches de pointe en matière de changements climatiques et de pollution de l'environnement et de leurs relations avec les questions pertinentes de déclin de la biodiversité et des services écosystémiques, tout en anticipant les orientations futures

Le forum international sur le changement climatique tenu à Sousse offre une occasion inestimable de présenter, discuter et mettre en pratique des idées et approches novatrices en matière de changement climatique, de pollution environnementale et de leur impact sur le déclin de la biodiversité et des services écosystémiques. Cet événement nous permet également d'anticiper les orientations futures pour mieux faire face à ces défis mondiaux. Rejoignez-nous pour échanger des connaissances, inspirer des actions concrètes et collaborer en vue d'un avenir plus durable. #ForumChangementClimatique #ProtectionEnvironnement #Biodiversité

pot facebook in english: L’Université de Sousse, le laboratoire « Agrobiodivesrité et Ecotoxiologie » de l’ISA-CM et l’Association Tunisienne d’Ecotoxicologie et d’Ecophysiologie (ATEE) organisent le forum sur les “Changements Climatiques et la Pollution de l'Environnement”, qui se tiendra à Sousse, du 2 au 4 novembre 2023 à l’Hôtel El Mouradi Palace (Zone Kantaoui). 
Le forum international sur le changement climatique tenu à Sousse offre une occasion inestimable de présenter, discuter et mettre en pratique des idées et approches novatrices en matière de changement climatique, de pollution environnementale et de leur impact sur le déclin de la biodiversité et des services écosystémiques. Cet événement nous permet également d’anticiper les orientations futures pour mieux faire face à ces défis mondiaux. 
Un programme scientifique attractif comprenant différentes sessions scientifiques. 
Rejoignez-nous pour échanger des connaissances, inspirer des actions concrètes et collaborer en vue d’un avenir plus durable. #ForumChangementClimatique #ProtectionEnvironnement #Biodiversité

The University of Sousse, the "Agrobiodiversity and Ecotoxicology" laboratory at ISA-CM, and the Tunisian Association of Ecotoxicology and Ecophysiology (ATEE) are organizing the "Climate Change and Environmental Pollution" forum, which will be held in Sousse from November 2 to 4, 2023 at El Mouradi Palace Hotel (Kantaoui Zone).
The International Forum on Climate Change held in Sousse provides an invaluable opportunity to showcase, discuss, and implement innovative ideas and approaches in climate change, environmental pollution, and their impact on biodiversity decline and ecosystem services. This event also allows us to anticipate future directions to better address these global challenges.
An attractive scientific program including various scientific sessions will be featured.
Join us to exchange knowledge, inspire concrete actions, and collaborate towards a more sustainable future. #ClimateChangeForum #EnvironmentalProtection #Biodiversity
meseare of impact of online webinars versus in situ events

With the rise of technology and the increasing accessibility of the internet, online webinars have become a popular alternative to in-person events. However, it is important to consider the impact of each format.

Online webinars offer several advantages. First and foremost, they provide a cost-effective solution as there is no need for travel expenses or venue rentals. This allows for greater reach and inclusivity, as people from all around the world can participate without the limitations of geographical distance. Online webinars also provide flexibility, as participants can join from the comfort of their own homes or offices, eliminating the need for time-consuming travel arrangements.

On the other hand, in-situ events have their own unique benefits. They offer the opportunity for face-to-face interactions and networking. In-person events allow for a more immersive experience, where attendees can engage in informal discussions, ask questions directly to speakers, and build personal connections with others in their field. Additionally, being physically present at an event may create a stronger sense of engagement and motivation for participants.

Both online webinars and in-situ events have their place in knowledge sharing and fostering collaborations. The choice between the two depends on the specific goals of the event and the needs of the target audience. Hybrid formats, combining aspects of both online and in-person interactions, can also be considered to leverage the advantages of both formats.

Ultimately, the impact of online webinars versus in-situ events will vary depending on the context and objectives. It is crucial to carefully assess the goals, resources, target audience, and desired outcomes when deciding on the appropriate format for an event.

The impact of online webinars versus in situ events is a topic that has gained significant attention, especially in recent times with the widespread adoption of virtual platforms for knowledge sharing. 

Online webinars have the advantage of being accessible to a wider audience without the limitations of physical location. This means participants from different parts of the world can join and benefit from the content being shared. Furthermore, online webinars can be recorded and made available for on-demand viewing, offering flexibility for individuals who may not be able to attend the live session.

In terms of environmental impact, online webinars have a significantly lower carbon footprint compared to in situ events. With no need for travel and reduced energy consumption associated with venues, virtual events contribute to a more sustainable approach.

However, in situ events offer unique benefits as well. They provide face-to-face interactions and networking opportunities, allowing participants to engage in meaningful discussions, build personal connections, and foster collaborations. In-person events often create a more immersive and memorable experience for attendees.

Another factor to consider is the level of engagement and attention during online webinars. With distractions at home or office environments, participants may find it challenging to fully focus on the session. In situ events, on the other hand, offer a dedicated environment where attendees are more likely to devote their attention to the presentations and discussions.

Overall, both online webinars and in situ events have their advantages and limitations. The choice between the two depends on various factors including the desired level of interaction, the target audience, accessibility, resources, and the specific goals of the event. Considering these factors, a combination of online and in situ formats may provide the best results to reach a diverse and engaged audience while maximizing the impact of the event.

Given that the main objective of inforessessions is to reach the most useful target audience possible (people or organisations who are already beneficiaries or who are potential beneficiaries), online inforessessions seem to be a very appropriate solution for reaching parties. in addition, the focal point can mobilise their local network and partners.

Absolutely! Online inforessessions can be a highly effective solution for reaching the target audience, especially when the focus is on existing or potential beneficiaries. The convenience and accessibility of virtual platforms allow for a broader reach, as individuals and organizations can join from any location, without the need for travel or geographical limitations. This means that previously untapped or remote audiences can easily participate and benefit from the information being shared.

Furthermore, by organizing online inforessessions, the focal point or organizer can leverage their local network and partners to spread the word and engage a larger audience. They can actively promote the event through social media, email marketing, and other channels, ensuring that the relevant parties are aware of and have the opportunity to participate.

Online inforessessions also provide the opportunity for interactive features such as Q&A sessions, polls, and chat forums, which can enhance engagement and facilitate direct communication between the focal point and the audience. This level of accessibility and interactivity can foster a stronger connection with the target audience and enable a more targeted and tailored approach to addressing their specific needs and concerns.

Overall, online inforessessions offer a cost-effective, convenient, and flexible solution to reaching the most useful target audience. By utilizing virtual platforms and actively mobilizing local networks and partners, the impact and reach of these sessions can be maximized. This approach ensures that the right information reaches the right people or organizations, increasing the chances of successful engagement and potential beneficiary outcomes.

enrich this: the results from surveys as well as profiling analysis showed that few ENFPs have the competence and the skills  to lead an in situ information session, namely: South Africa, Burkina Faso, Republic of Congo and Senegal

The results from surveys and profiling analysis have provided valuable insights regarding the competence and skills required to lead in situ information sessions. Specifically, it has been observed that among the surveyed countries, namely South Africa, Burkina Faso, Republic of Congo, and Senegal, there are relatively few individuals with the ENFP personality type who possess the necessary qualities for effective leadership in such sessions.

ENFPs are known for their enthusiasm, creativity, and strong interpersonal skills. These traits can greatly contribute to engaging and inspiring participants in an in situ setting. However, the analysis suggests a limited number of ENFPs in the mentioned countries possess the specific competence and skills required to effectively lead information sessions.

While this may present some challenges, it also highlights an opportunity for targeted capacity building and development programs. By identifying and nurturing individuals with the potential to become successful in situ information session leaders, organizations and stakeholders can strive to bridge this gap and ensure the availability of competent and skilled individuals for future sessions.

Moreover, collaboration and knowledge sharing within these countries, as well as with other countries that have demonstrable success in this area, can be an effective strategy to enhance the capabilities of potential leaders in in situ information sessions. This exchange of experiences, best practices, and capacity-building initiatives can foster the development of a strong and diverse pool of leaders capable of effectively delivering in situ information sessions across various contexts.

In conclusion, while the survey and profiling analysis identified a scarcity of ENFPs with the required competence and skills in South Africa, Burkina Faso, Republic of Congo, and Senegal for leading in situ information sessions, this presents an opportunity for targeted capacity building efforts and cross-country collaboration. By investing in the development of capable leaders and fostering knowledge exchange, we can work towards the successful implementation of in situ information sessions in these countries and beyond.

The results from surveys and profiling analysis have provided valuable insights regarding the competence and skills required to lead in situ information sessions. The analysis specifically highlights that in several countries, including South Africa, Burkina Faso, Republic of Congo, and Senegal, there is a limited number of individuals with the ENFP personality type who possess the necessary qualities for effective leadership in these sessions.

ENFPs are typically known for their enthusiasm, creativity, and strong interpersonal skills, which are valuable traits in engaging and inspiring participants in an in situ setting. However, the analysis indicates a scarcity of ENFPs in the mentioned countries who possess the specific competence and skills needed for leading successful information sessions.

This finding emphasizes the importance of targeted capacity building and skills development initiatives. By identifying individuals with leadership potential and supporting their professional growth, organizations and stakeholders can help bridge the gap and ensure a capable and skilled workforce for future in situ information sessions.

Creating avenues for collaboration and knowledge exchange among countries facing similar challenges can also be instrumental in enhancing the capabilities of potential leaders. By sharing experiences, best practices, and implementing joint capacity-building programs, these countries can leverage each other's strengths and work towards building a diverse and dynamic pool of leaders equipped to deliver impactful in situ information sessions.

In conclusion, the scarcity of ENFPs with the necessary competence and skills in South Africa, Burkina Faso, Republic of Congo, and Senegal for leading in situ information sessions highlights the need for targeted capacity-building efforts. Through collaboration and knowledge sharing, we can empower individuals with leadership potential and foster the development of a skilled workforce dedicated to delivering successful in situ information sessions in these countries and beyond.
